page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, operating on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel processor with 32 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It features advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the n4-highcpu-64 demonstrates strong performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. Its Geekbench multi-core score, software compilation, and ray tracing capabilities rank in the top 25% of tested systems. Image processing and static web serving throughput reach top-tier levels, placing in the top 10% of benchmarks. Database operations via Passmark also achieve top-tier status. For large language model inference, the instance delivers strong prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium models, though text generation for large 70B models falls into the average tier. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, while large block read and write speeds remain average.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, minimizing expenditures on unneeded RAM. However, the allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. The complete absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. This server is best suited for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, image processing, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel processor with 32 physical cores. It lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in static web serving, image processing, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core execution and software compilation speeds. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive tasks, but the hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for web serving, compilation, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance in web serving, database operations, and image processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier web serving performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring advanced matrix extensions and high-density processing power for compute-bound workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM on x86_64 Intel architecture
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Top-tier performance in static web serving, image processing, and database operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based model inference
